Eigent AI: The World's First Multi-Agent Workforce Platform

Jan 4, 2026
Vibe Sparking

Eigent AI: The World’s First Multi-Agent Workforce Platform

Section titled “Eigent AI: The World’s First Multi-Agent Workforce Platform”

Introduction: The New Productivity Engine

Section titled “Introduction: The New Productivity Engine”

In the rapidly evolving landscape of AI automation, Eigent emerges as the world’s first Multi-Agent Workforce desktop application. Built on the robust foundation of CAMEL-AI, Eigent empowers users to build, manage, and deploy a custom AI workforce that transforms complex workflows into automated tasks.

Build your own AI Agent Workforce with Eigent — a non-intrusive integration that offers adaptable, ever-improving performance for any scenario, letting AI Workers run your operations automatically.

Key Features

Section titled “Key Features”

🏭 Multi-Agent Workforce

Section titled “🏭 Multi-Agent Workforce”

Eigent employs a team of specialized AI agents that collaborate to solve complex tasks. The platform dynamically breaks down tasks and activates multiple agents to work in parallel, significantly boosting productivity through coordinated effort.

Pre-defined Agent Workers:

  • Developer Agent: Writes and executes code, runs terminal commands
  • Search Agent: Searches the web and extracts content
  • Document Agent: Creates and manages documents
  • Multi-Modal Agent: Processes images and audio

🔌 MCP Tools Integration

Section titled “🔌 MCP Tools Integration”

Eigent comes with massive built-in Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools and allows you to install your own custom tools. Equip agents with exactly the right tools for your scenarios — even integrate internal APIs or custom functions.

Built-in MCP Tools Include:

  • Web browsing capabilities
  • Code execution environments
  • Notion integration
  • Google Suite connectivity
  • Slack integration
  • And many more…

🎯 Infinitely Customizable

Section titled “🎯 Infinitely Customizable”

Build your own worker nodes to execute any task. With pluggable MCPs, you have full control to create agents with the perfect tools for the job. Your workforce, your rules.

🔒 Private and Secure by Design

Section titled “🔒 Private and Secure by Design”

Your data stays yours, always. With a focus on local, private deployment, your workflows and sensitive information never leave your control. Eigent is open-source for full transparency and trust.

Getting Started

Section titled “Getting Started”

☁️ Cloud Version

Section titled “☁️ Cloud Version”

The fastest way to experience Eigent’s multi-agent AI capabilities is through their cloud platform:

  • Instant Access - Start building multi-agent workflows in minutes
  • Managed Infrastructure - They handle scaling, updates, and maintenance
  • Premium Support - Subscribe and get priority assistance from the engineering team

Get started at Eigent.ai →

🏠 Self-Hosting (Community Edition)

Section titled “🏠 Self-Hosting (Community Edition)”

For users who prefer local control, data privacy, or customization:

Prerequisites:

  • Node.js (version 18-22) and npm

Benefits of Self-Hosting:

  • Data Privacy - Keep sensitive data within your infrastructure
  • Customization - Modify and extend the platform to fit your needs
  • Cost Control - Avoid recurring cloud fees for large-scale deployments

🏢 Enterprise

Section titled “🏢 Enterprise”

For organizations requiring maximum security, customization, and control:

  • Commercial License available
  • Exclusive Features (like SSO & custom development)
  • Scalable Enterprise Deployment
  • Negotiated SLAs & implementation services

Contact: info@eigent.ai

Real-World Use Cases

Section titled “Real-World Use Cases”

1. Travel Planning

Section titled “1. Travel Planning”

Prompt: “We are two tennis fans and want to go see the tennis tournament in Palm Springs 2026. I live in SF - please prepare a detailed itinerary with flights, hotels, things to do for 3 days - around the time semifinal/finals are happening. We like hiking, vegan food and spas. Our budget is $5K.”

Eigent successfully planned a detailed 3-day tennis trip from SF to Palm Springs with flights, hotels, vegan food, hiking, spas, and finals tickets — all within a $5K budget, ending with a report + Slack message.

2. Financial Reporting

Section titled “2. Financial Reporting”

Prompt: “Please help me prepare a Q2 financial statement based on my bank transfer record file bank_transaction.csv in my desktop to a html report with chart to investors how much we have spent.”

Eigent processed CSV bank data and created an investor-ready HTML report with Q2 spending summary and charts.

3. Market Research

Section titled “3. Market Research”

Prompt: “Analyze the UK healthcare industry to support the planning of my next company. Provide a comprehensive market overview, including current trends, growth projections, and relevant regulations.”

Eigent delivered comprehensive market analysis with trends, regulations, growth projections, and identified top opportunities in professional HTML format.

4. Social Media Automation

Section titled “4. Social Media Automation”

Using customized Twitter MCP and Twitter Agent powered by open-source self-deployed models to help get information from public online posts then send tweets automatically.

5. Data Extraction

Section titled “5. Data Extraction”

Eigent collected all stargazers of the eigent-ai/eigent repository and built a table containing each stargazer’s GitHub username, name, GitHub profile URL, and public LinkedIn and X (Twitter) links found on their GitHub profile pages.

Tech Stack

Section titled “Tech Stack”

Backend

Section titled “Backend”
  • Framework: FastAPI
  • Package Manager: uv
  • Async Server: Uvicorn
  • Authentication: OAuth 2.0, Passlib
  • Multi-agent framework: CAMEL

Frontend

Section titled “Frontend”
  • Framework: React
  • Desktop App Framework: Electron
  • Language: TypeScript
  • UI: Tailwind CSS, Radix UI, Lucide React, Framer Motion
  • State Management: Zustand
  • Flow Editor: React Flow

Development Roadmap

Section titled “Development Roadmap”
TopicsDescriptionDiscord Channel
Context EngineeringPrompt caching, System prompt optimize, Toolkit docstring optimize, Context compressionJoin Discord
Multi-modal EnhancementMore accurate image understanding when using browser, Advanced video generationJoin Discord
Multi-agent systemWorkforce support fixed workflow, Workforce support multi-round conversionJoin Discord
Browser ToolkitBrowseCamp integration, Benchmark improvement, Forbid repeated page visiting, Automatic cache button clickingJoin Discord
Document ToolkitSupport dynamic file editingJoin Discord
Terminal ToolkitBenchmark improvement, Terminal-Bench integrationJoin Discord
Environment & RLEnvironment design, Data-generation, RL framework integration (VERL, TRL, OpenRLHF)Join Discord

Why Choose Eigent?

Section titled “Why Choose Eigent?”

✅ Zero Setup

Section titled “✅ Zero Setup”

No technical configuration required — download and start building your AI workforce.

✅ Multi-Agent Coordination

Section titled “✅ Multi-Agent Coordination”

Handle complex multi-agent workflows with intelligent task distribution.

✅ Enterprise Features

Section titled “✅ Enterprise Features”

SSO/Access control for organizational deployment.

✅ Local Deployment

Section titled “✅ Local Deployment”

Keep your data private with full local deployment capabilities.

✅ 100% Open Source

Section titled “✅ 100% Open Source”

Full transparency — download, inspect, and modify the code.

✅ Custom Model Support

Section titled “✅ Custom Model Support”

Deploy with your preferred models, whether cloud or local.

✅ MCP Integration

Section titled “✅ MCP Integration”

Massive ecosystem of tools with custom tool installation support.

Community & Support

Section titled “Community & Support”
  • GitHub: eigent-ai/eigent — Report bugs, request features, and track development
  • Discord: Join Discord — Get real-time support, chat with the community
  • X (Twitter): @Eigent_AI — Follow for updates, AI insights, and announcements
  • Documentation: docs.eigent.ai — Comprehensive guides and API reference

Licensing

Section titled “Licensing”

Eigent is licensed under the Eigent Open Source License, based on Apache 2.0 with additional conditions. For enterprise licensing inquiries, contact info@eigent.ai.

Conclusion

Section titled “Conclusion”

Eigent represents a paradigm shift in AI automation — moving beyond single-agent chatbots to a true multi-agent workforce platform. Whether you’re an individual looking to automate personal workflows or an enterprise seeking scalable AI solutions, Eigent provides the tools, flexibility, and security to build your perfect AI workforce.

Liked this article? Follow for more insights on AI automation, multi-agent systems, and the future of work.

References:

  • Eigent Official Website
  • Eigent GitHub Repository
  • CAMEL-AI Framework
Tags:
  • multi-agent
  • AI-workforce
  • CAMEL-AI
  • MCP
  • automation

Ralph Wiggum: The Official Claude Code Plugin for Autonomous AI Development Loops

Jan 3, 2026
Vibe Sparking

Ralph Wiggum: The Official Claude Code Plugin for Autonomous AI Development Loops

Section titled “Ralph Wiggum: The Official Claude Code Plugin for Autonomous AI Development Loops”

Introduction: Ralph is Just a Bash Loop

Section titled “Introduction: Ralph is Just a Bash Loop”

In the world of AI-assisted development, a fascinating technique has emerged from the mind of Geoffrey Huntley. Named after the famously persistent Ralph Wiggum from The Simpsons, the Ralph Wiggum technique embodies a simple yet powerful philosophy: keep trying until you succeed.

As Huntley famously describes it:

“Ralph is a Bash loop” - a simple while true that repeatedly feeds an AI agent a prompt file, allowing it to iteratively improve its work until completion.

Now, Anthropic has released an official Claude Code plugin that implements this technique, bringing autonomous AI development loops to your fingertips.

Understanding the Ralph Wiggum Technique

Section titled “Understanding the Ralph Wiggum Technique”

Core Concept

Section titled “Core Concept”

The Ralph Wiggum technique is built on the principle of persistent iteration despite setbacks. Instead of trying to get everything right in a single shot, you let the AI fail, learn from its failures, and try again - automatically.

The traditional approach to AI-assisted development looks like this:

The Ralph Wiggum approach flips this:

How It Works Under the Hood

Section titled “How It Works Under the Hood”

The official ralph-wiggum plugin implements this using a Stop hook that intercepts Claude’s exit attempts:

The loop happens inside your current session - no external bash loops needed. The Stop hook in hooks/stop-hook.sh creates the self-referential feedback loop.

Key Features of the Official Plugin

Section titled “Key Features of the Official Plugin”

1. Self-Referential Feedback Loop

Section titled “1. Self-Referential Feedback Loop”
  • The prompt never changes between iterations
  • Claude’s previous work persists in files
  • Each iteration sees modified files and git history
  • Claude autonomously improves by reading its own past work

2. Simple Command Interface

Section titled “2. Simple Command Interface”

The plugin provides two main commands:

Starting a loop:

Canceling a loop:

3. Safety Controls

Section titled “3. Safety Controls”
  • --max-iterations <n> - Prevents infinite loops by stopping after N iterations
  • --completion-promise "<text>" - Defines the exact phrase that signals completion

Best Practices for Writing Ralph Prompts

Section titled “Best Practices for Writing Ralph Prompts”

The success of the Ralph Wiggum technique depends heavily on how you write your prompts.

❌ Bad Examples

Section titled “❌ Bad Examples”

✅ Good Examples

Section titled “✅ Good Examples”

When to Use Ralph Wiggum

Section titled “When to Use Ralph Wiggum”

✅ Good For

Section titled “✅ Good For”
  • Well-defined tasks with clear success criteria
  • Tasks requiring iteration (e.g., getting tests to pass)
  • Greenfield projects where you can walk away
  • Tasks with automatic verification (tests, linters)

❌ Not Good For

Section titled “❌ Not Good For”
  • Tasks requiring human judgment or design decisions
  • One-shot operations
  • Tasks with unclear success criteria
  • Production debugging (use targeted debugging instead)

Real-World Results

Section titled “Real-World Results”

The Ralph Wiggum technique has demonstrated impressive real-world outcomes:

  • Successfully generated 6 repositories overnight in Y Combinator hackathon testing
  • One $50k contract completed for $297 in API costs
  • Created an entire programming language (“cursed”) over 3 months using this approach

Philosophy Behind the Technique

Section titled “Philosophy Behind the Technique”

Ralph embodies several key principles that make it effective:

  1. Don’t aim for perfect on first try - Let the loop refine the work
  2. “Deterministically bad” means failures are predictable - Use them to tune prompts
  3. Success depends on writing good prompts - Not just having a good model
  4. Keep trying until success - The loop handles retry logic automatically

Installation and Setup

Section titled “Installation and Setup”

The Ralph Wiggum plugin is available in the official Anthropic Claude plugins repository:

Key files in the plugin:

  • .claude-plugin - Plugin configuration
  • commands/ - Command definitions (/ralph-loop, /cancel-ralph)
  • hooks/stop-hook.sh - The magic that makes the loop work
  • scripts/ - Supporting scripts

Advanced Usage: Ralph Orchestrator

Section titled “Advanced Usage: Ralph Orchestrator”

For more complex workflows, the Ralph Orchestrator provides enhanced functionality:

  • Web-based interface for managing multiple Ralph loops
  • Parallel execution of multiple tasks
  • Progress monitoring and logging
  • Integration with CI/CD pipelines

Learn more about Ralph Orchestrator

Conclusion

Section titled “Conclusion”

The Ralph Wiggum plugin represents a paradigm shift in how we think about AI-assisted development. Instead of treating AI as a one-shot tool, it embraces the reality that software development is iterative - and AI should be too.

By letting Claude fail, learn, and try again automatically, we unlock a level of autonomy that wasn’t possible before. Whether you’re building a REST API, generating test suites, or working on a complex multi-phase project, Ralph Wiggum gives you the power to define the destination and let AI find the path.

References

Section titled “References”
  • Official GitHub Repository
  • Original Technique by Geoffrey Huntley
  • Ralph Orchestrator
  • Awesome Claude Guide
  • DeepWiki Documentation

Published: January 3, 2026 Author: VibeSparking AI Research Team

Tags:
  • Claude Code
  • AI Development
  • Automation
  • Plugins
  • Anthropic

Agentic Signal: Visual AI Workflow Automation Platform with Local LLM Integration

Dec 29, 2025
Vibe Sparking

Explore Agentic Signal by Code Forge Temple — a visual drag-and-drop AI workflow automation platform that runs entirely locally with Ollama integration. Build intelligent workflows without coding, with no cloud dependencies required.

Introduction

Section titled “Introduction”

Agentic Signal is an open-source visual AI workflow automation platform created by Code Forge Temple. It enables users to build intelligent, AI-powered workflows using a drag-and-drop interface while running everything locally on their machine.

What sets Agentic Signal apart is its commitment to privacy-first local execution. By integrating with Ollama, it allows you to harness the power of large language models without sending your data to the cloud. This makes it ideal for processing sensitive documents, building personal automation workflows, or simply maintaining full control over your AI interactions.

Repository: code-forge-temple/agentic-signal Website: agentic-signal.com

Key Features

Section titled “Key Features”
FeatureDescription
Visual Drag-and-Drop InterfaceBuild workflows visually using React Flow — no coding required
Node-Based ArchitectureConnect data sources, AI processors, and outputs seamlessly
Real-time ExecutionSee your workflows run in real-time with live data flow visualization
Ollama IntegrationRun AI models locally with privacy and control
Tool CallingAI agents can execute functions and access external APIs
Structured OutputJSON schema validation for reliable AI responses
Conversation MemoryMaintain context across workflow executions
Browser AutomationWeb scraping and automation via Google Chrome integration

Quick Start

Section titled “Quick Start”

Installation

Section titled “Installation”

Agentic Signal provides pre-built releases for Windows, macOS, and Linux:

  1. Download the release package for your operating system from GitHub Releases

  2. Install Google Chrome — required for web scraping and browser automation nodes

  3. Install and Run Agentic Signal

    • Run the installer and follow the prompts
    • Windows users: When launching for the first time, your antivirus may scan the app (the installer is unsigned)

  4. Configure Ollama

    • Make sure Ollama is running with CORS enabled for the Windows app
    • Configure the Ollama Host in app settings

Prerequisites

Section titled “Prerequisites”

Required

Section titled “Required”

  • Google Chrome — Required for browser automation and web scraping nodes

    • Download from google.com/chrome

  • Ollama — Local LLM runtime

Optional (for Google Services Integration)

Section titled “Optional (for Google Services Integration)”

If you want to use Gmail, Google Drive, or Google Calendar nodes, you’ll need to set up OAuth2 credentials:

  1. Go to Google Cloud Console
  2. Create a new project or select existing one
  3. Enable the APIs you want to use (Gmail, Drive, Calendar)
  4. Create OAuth 2.0 credentials (Desktop app)
  5. Download the credentials JSON file

Node Types and Architecture

Section titled “Node Types and Architecture”

Agentic Signal uses a node-based workflow architecture where each node represents a processing unit:

Node Structure

Section titled “Node Structure”
  • Input/Output Data Connectors — Connect data flow between nodes
  • Tool Connector — Additional connectors for AI tools and functions
  • Timer Connector — Schedule automatic execution at intervals
  • Settings — Configure node-specific options
  • Error Logs — View errors when processing fails
  • Output — View processed results
  • Run — Manually trigger workflow execution

Available Node Types

Section titled “Available Node Types”
Node TypeDescription
Data SourceInput data from files, APIs, or manual entry
AI Data ProcessingProcess data with local LLMs via Ollama
AI ToolExecute function calling with AI agents
HTTP DataMake REST API calls to external services
JSON ReformatterTransform and restructure JSON data
ChartVisualize data with various chart types
Data ValidationValidate data against schemas
Data Flow SpyDebug and inspect data flow
Timer NodeSchedule periodic executions

Integrations

Section titled “Integrations”

Agentic Signal supports a growing list of integrations:

Built-in Integrations

Section titled “Built-in Integrations”
IntegrationDescription
Ollama LLMsUse local language models for text analysis and generation
Google ServicesGmail, Google Drive, Google Calendar (with OAuth2 setup)
Weather APIsReal-time weather data
Search EnginesDuckDuckGo, Brave Search
Date/Time ToolsGet current date and time
HTTP/REST APIsIntegrate any REST API via HTTP Data node

Browser Automation

Section titled “Browser Automation”
  • Web scraping capabilities
  • Form filling automation
  • Data extraction from websites
  • Screenshot capture

Daily Workflow Examples

Section titled “Daily Workflow Examples”

Example 1: AI-Powered Email Assistant

Section titled “Example 1: AI-Powered Email Assistant”

Create a workflow that:

  1. Fetches new emails from Gmail (Data Source → Gmail)
  2. Uses local LLM to categorize and prioritize (AI Data Processing)
  3. Generates summary responses (AI Tool)
  4. Saves to Google Drive for reference (HTTP Data)

Example 2: Research Automation

Section titled “Example 2: Research Automation”

Build a workflow that:

  1. Takes a research topic as input (Data Source)
  2. Searches multiple sources (DuckDuckGo, Brave Search nodes)
  3. Extracts and summarizes findings with local LLM (AI Data Processing)
  4. Formats results into a structured report (JSON Reformatter)
  5. Saves to file or sends to email (Output node)

Example 3: Weather-Based Notifications

Section titled “Example 3: Weather-Based Notifications”

Create a daily automation:

  1. Trigger at 7 AM daily (Timer Node)
  2. Fetch weather data for your location (Weather API)
  3. Generate personalized recommendation with local LLM (AI Data Processing)
  4. Display formatted output (Chart or Display node)

Configuration

Section titled “Configuration”

Ollama Configuration

Section titled “Ollama Configuration”

For the desktop app to communicate with Ollama:

Then in Agentic Signal app settings:

  • Set Ollama Host to http://localhost:11434
  • Select your preferred model from the dropdown

Chrome Path Configuration

Section titled “Chrome Path Configuration”

For browser automation nodes, configure the Chrome executable path:

  • Windows: C:\Program Files\Google\Chrome\Application\chrome.exe
  • macOS: /Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome
  • Linux: /usr/bin/google-chrome

Comparison with Similar Tools

Section titled “Comparison with Similar Tools”
FeatureAgentic Signaln8nLangFlowFlowise
Local LLM Support✅ Native Ollama✅ With setup✅✅
No-Code Interface✅ Drag-and-drop✅✅✅
Privacy-First✅ Fully local⚠️ Hybrid⚠️ Setup needed⚠️ Setup needed
Browser Automation✅ Built-in✅❌❌
Structured Output✅ JSON schema✅✅✅
Tool Calling✅✅✅✅
LicenseDual (AGPL/Commercial)Fair CodeApache 2.0Apache 2.0

Use Cases

Section titled “Use Cases”

For Individuals

Section titled “For Individuals”
  • Personal Knowledge Management — Process and organize notes, documents, and web content
  • Task Automation — Automate repetitive tasks with AI intelligence
  • Research Assistant — Gather, analyze, and summarize information from multiple sources
  • Email Management — Categorize, prioritize, and draft responses

For Developers

Section titled “For Developers”
  • API Testing — Visual workflow builder for API integrations
  • Data Processing Pipelines — Transform and validate data with AI assistance
  • Automation Scripts — Replace complex scripts with visual workflows
  • Prototyping — Quickly test AI workflow concepts

For Businesses

Section titled “For Businesses”
  • Document Processing — Extract and analyze information from documents
  • Customer Support — Automate response drafting and categorization
  • Data Analysis — Generate insights from structured and unstructured data
  • Workflow Automation — Connect multiple services and APIs

Troubleshooting

Section titled “Troubleshooting”

App Won’t Start (Windows)

Section titled “App Won’t Start (Windows)”

If the app is slow or unresponsive on first launch:

  1. Wait for antivirus scan to complete
  2. Close and restart Agentic Signal
  3. Add exception to antivirus if needed

Ollama Connection Failed

Section titled “Ollama Connection Failed”
  1. Verify Ollama is running: ollama list
  2. Check CORS is enabled: OLLAMA_ORIGINS="*" ollama serve
  3. Verify Ollama Host in app settings is http://localhost:11434

Browser Automation Not Working

Section titled “Browser Automation Not Working”
  1. Verify Chrome is installed
  2. Check Chrome executable path in app settings
  3. Make sure Chrome version is up-to-date

Best Practices

Section titled “Best Practices”
  1. Start Simple — Begin with basic workflows before adding complexity
  2. Use Appropriate Models — Choose Ollama models based on your task (faster models for simple tasks, more capable for complex reasoning)
  3. Modular Design — Break complex workflows into smaller, reusable components
  4. Test Iteratively — Run and test each node as you build
  5. Monitor Performance — Use Data Flow Spy to debug bottlenecks

Community and Support

Section titled “Community and Support”
  • Documentation: https://agentic-signal.com/
  • GitHub: https://github.com/code-forge-temple/agentic-signal
  • Discord: Join the Agentic Signal Discord
  • Reddit: r/AgenticSignal
  • Issues: GitHub Issues
  • Discussions: GitHub Discussions

License

Section titled “License”

Dual License Model

  • Open Source (AGPL v3): Free for personal, educational, and non-commercial use
  • Commercial License: Required for business use, SaaS, or proprietary integration

See LICENSE.md for full details.

Sponsor This Project

Section titled “Sponsor This Project”

If you find Agentic Signal valuable, consider supporting development:

  • GitHub Sponsors: Sponsor code-forge-temple
  • Star the Repository: ⭐ GitHub

References

Section titled “References”
  1. Agentic Signal Official Website
  2. Agentic Signal GitHub Repository
  3. Ollama - Local LLM Runtime
  4. Quick Start Guide
  5. Nodes Reference
  6. Build AI Workflows Visually - dev.to Article
  7. Demo Video on YouTube

Published: December 29, 2025

Tags:
  • AI Workflow
  • Ollama
  • Local LLM
  • React Flow
  • Privacy-First
  • No-Code
  • Automation

WeChat Article Writer: Automate WeChat Public Account Content with Claude Code

Dec 28, 2025
Vibe Sparking

WeChat Article Writer: Automate WeChat Public Account Content with Claude Code

Section titled “WeChat Article Writer: Automate WeChat Public Account Content with Claude Code”

Creating engaging content for WeChat public accounts (公众号) is a time-consuming process that involves research, writing, headline optimization, and formatting. The wechat-article-writer skill for Claude Code automates this entire workflow, allowing content creators to produce professional articles in minutes rather than hours.

What is wechat-article-writer?

Section titled “What is wechat-article-writer?”

wechat-article-writer is a Claude Code skill from the happy-claude-skills collection that automates the entire WeChat public account article creation process. It handles:

  • Research - Multi-source web search for latest information
  • Writing - AI-powered content generation with storytelling elements
  • Headlines - Viral title generation with proven formulas
  • Formatting - Professional layout optimized for mobile reading

GitHub Repository: iamzhihuix/happy-claude-skills License: MIT

The 4-Step Workflow

Section titled “The 4-Step Workflow”

Step 1: Research (搜索资料)

Section titled “Step 1: Research (搜索资料)”

The skill automatically searches multiple sources in parallel:

  • Official documentation
  • Social media (X/Twitter, Reddit)
  • Technical forums and communities
  • Latest monthly/quarterly content

It uses Claude Code’s built-in WebSearch tool and can spawn parallel tasks to accelerate information gathering.

Step 2: Writing (撰写文章)

Section titled “Step 2: Writing (撰写文章)”

Content generation follows best practices for WeChat articles:

  • Length: 1000-1500 words (optimal for mobile reading)
  • Opening: Story-driven with emotional hooks (excitement/anxiety/curiosity)
  • Structure: Effect showcase → Problem description → Tutorial → Conclusion
  • Style: Reads your CLAUDE.md to match your personal writing style

Step 3: Viral Headlines (生成标题)

Section titled “Step 3: Viral Headlines (生成标题)”

Generates 5 viral headlines using proven techniques:

  • Pain points: “Still doing X manually?”
  • Numbers: “3 minutes”, “5 tips” - specific digits are persuasive
  • Results: “Efficiency up 10x”
  • Emotion: “Amazing”, “Secret weapon”, “Ultimate guide”
  • Curiosity: Headlines that make readers want to click

Step 4: Formatting (排版优化)

Section titled “Step 4: Formatting (排版优化)”

Mobile-optimized layout recommendations:

  • Short paragraphs (3-5 lines each)
  • Key data points in bold, standalone paragraphs
  • Strategic image placement (cover, step illustrations, conclusion)
  • Code blocks with proper spacing and syntax highlighting
  • “Golden quotes” - memorable phrases set apart for impact

Installation

Section titled “Installation”

Prerequisites

Section titled “Prerequisites”
  • Claude Code installed
  • Access to Claude Skills feature

Install from Repository

Section titled “Install from Repository”

Usage

Section titled “Usage”

Once installed, simply describe your article needs:

Claude Code will automatically:

  1. Detect the request matches the wechat-article-writer skill
  2. Search for latest AI programming trends and tips
  3. Read your CLAUDE.md to match your writing style
  4. Generate a 1200-word tutorial article
  5. Provide 5 viral headline options
  6. Suggest formatting and image placement

Example Workflow

Section titled “Example Workflow”

Input:

Output:

  1. Research Summary: Latest Claude Code updates from official docs, Twitter discussions, and community forums
  2. Article: 1200-word tutorial with story-driven opening
  3. 5 Headlines:
    • “Still manually copying code? This Claude feature will change your life”
    • “3 minutes to master Claude Code’s hidden superpower”
    • “Productivity up 10x: The Claude Code feature pros don’t talk about”
    • “Amazing! Claude Code can now do this automatically”
    • “The ultimate Claude Code workflow nobody knows about”
  4. Layout Guide: Image placement suggestions, formatting recommendations

Key Features

Section titled “Key Features”

Multi-Source Research

Section titled “Multi-Source Research”

The skill searches across multiple platforms in parallel, ensuring your content is well-researched and up-to-date.

Personalized Writing Style

Section titled “Personalized Writing Style”

By reading your CLAUDE.md file, the skill adapts to your established voice and tone, maintaining consistency across your content.

Viral Optimization

Section titled “Viral Optimization”

Headline generation uses data-backed formulas from successful WeChat articles, maximizing click-through rates.

Mobile-First Formatting

Section titled “Mobile-First Formatting”

Layout recommendations are specifically designed for WeChat’s mobile interface, where most readers consume content.

Use Cases

Section titled “Use Cases”
  • Tech Bloggers: Quick turnaround on trending topics
  • Marketing Teams: Consistent brand voice across all content
  • Individual Creators: Scale content production without sacrificing quality
  • News Aggregators: Curate and synthesize information efficiently

Project Structure

Section titled “Project Structure”

Contributing

Section titled “Contributing”

The happy-claude-skills project is open source. Contributions are welcome:

  1. Fork the repository
  2. Create a feature branch
  3. Submit a pull request

Repository: https://github.com/iamzhihuix/happy-claude-skills

Comparison with Alternatives

Section titled “Comparison with Alternatives”
Featurewechat-article-writerManual WritingOther AI Tools
Research automation✓ Multi-source✗ Manual✗ Limited
WeChat optimization✓ Native✓ Manual✗ Generic
Style adaptation✓ CLAUDE.mdN/A✗ Fixed
Headline formulas✓ 5 viral options✗ Manual✗ Basic
Integration✓ Claude Code nativeN/A✗ Copy/paste

Limitations

Section titled “Limitations”
  • Requires Claude Code and access to Claude Skills
  • Chinese-language optimized (WeChat specific)
  • Output quality depends on search result availability
  • Requires manual review and editing for best results

Future Enhancements

Section titled “Future Enhancements”

Potential improvements being considered:

  • Direct publishing to WeChat API
  • Multi-language support beyond Chinese
  • Image generation integration
  • Analytics tracking integration

Conclusion

Section titled “Conclusion”

The wechat-article-writer skill transforms WeChat content creation from a multi-hour process into a streamlined workflow. By combining research, writing, headline optimization, and formatting into a single automated flow, it helps creators maintain consistency and quality while scaling production.

For Claude Code users who manage WeChat public accounts or create content in Chinese, this skill is a valuable addition to the toolkit.

Links

Section titled “Links”
  • GitHub: iamzhihuix/happy-claude-skills
  • Claude Code: https://claude.ai/code
  • Claude Skills Documentation: https://support.claude.com/en/articles/12512180-using-skills-in-claude

Desktop Commander MCP: Terminal Control & File System Access for Claude

Dec 22, 2025
Vibe Sparking

Desktop Commander MCP: Terminal Control & File System Access for Claude

Section titled “Desktop Commander MCP: Terminal Control & File System Access for Claude”

In the rapidly evolving AI development landscape, Desktop Commander MCP stands out as a game-changing tool that transforms Claude Desktop into a powerful desktop automation assistant. Unlike traditional IDE-focused tools, Desktop Commander provides a solution-centric approach that works with your entire operating system.

What is Desktop Commander MCP?

Section titled “What is Desktop Commander MCP?”

Desktop Commander MCP is an open-source MCP (Model Context Protocol) server created by wonderwhy-er that enables Claude to:

  • Execute terminal commands with output streaming
  • Search and manage file systems
  • Edit files with surgical precision using diff capabilities
  • Read and write Excel, PDF, and various data files
  • Run code in memory without file persistence

“Work with code and text, run processes and automate tasks beyond typical AI editor limitations.”

Key Features & Highlights

Section titled “Key Features & Highlights”

1. Terminal & Process Control

Section titled “1. Terminal & Process Control”

Desktop Commander provides comprehensive terminal management:

Capabilities include:

  • Command execution with configurable timeouts
  • Background process execution
  • Process listing and termination
  • Session management for SSH, databases, and development servers

2. Native Data File Support

Section titled “2. Native Data File Support”

One of Desktop Commander’s standout features is native file handling:

Excel Support (.xlsx, .xls, .xlsm):

  • Read, write, and edit spreadsheets
  • Search within Excel files
  • No external tools required

PDF Support:

  • Text extraction from PDF documents
  • PDF creation from markdown
  • Document modification capabilities

Data Analysis:

  • Instant CSV/JSON file analysis
  • Quick data exploration without setup

3. Advanced File Operations

Section titled “3. Advanced File Operations”

4. Surgical Code Editing

Section titled “4. Surgical Code Editing”

Desktop Commander offers two editing approaches:

  1. Targeted Replacements - For precise, surgical changes
  2. Full File Rewrites - For major modifications

Multi-file support with pattern-based replacements makes refactoring efficient.

5. In-Memory Code Execution

Section titled “5. In-Memory Code Execution”

Execute code without file persistence:

  • Python - Run Python scripts directly
  • Node.js - Execute JavaScript/TypeScript
  • R - Statistical computing support

6. Configuration Management

Section titled “6. Configuration Management”
  • Dynamic get/set configuration values
  • Batch settings updates
  • Changes without server restart

7. Audit Logging

Section titled “7. Audit Logging”
  • Automatic logging of all tool calls
  • 10MB log rotation limit
  • Detailed timestamps and arguments

Installation Options

Section titled “Installation Options”

Option 1: NPX (⭐ Recommended - Auto-Updates)

Section titled “Option 1: NPX (⭐ Recommended - Auto-Updates)”

Option 2: Bash Installer (macOS)

Section titled “Option 2: Bash Installer (macOS)”

Option 3: Smithery

Section titled “Option 3: Smithery”

Visit smithery.ai/server/@wonderwhy-er/desktop-commander

Option 4: Manual Configuration

Section titled “Option 4: Manual Configuration”

Add to claude_desktop_config.json:

Config file locations:

  • macOS: ~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json
  • Windows: %APPDATA%\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json
  • Linux: ~/.config/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json

Option 5: Local Checkout

Section titled “Option 5: Local Checkout”

Option 6: Docker 🐳 (No Node.js Required)

Section titled “Option 6: Docker 🐳 (No Node.js Required)”

Docker Configuration:

Recommended Scenarios & Best Practices

Section titled “Recommended Scenarios & Best Practices”

1. Development Workflow Automation

Section titled “1. Development Workflow Automation”

Real user feedback:

“I had 76 errors in 23 files in my Svelte 5 project. Used desktop-commander, sequentialthinking, and tree-sitter to fix them all. Never resolved type errors this quickly with AI before!“

2. Data Analysis Pipeline

Section titled “2. Data Analysis Pipeline”

3. System Administration

Section titled “3. System Administration”

4. Codebase Exploration

Section titled “4. Codebase Exploration”

5. Sandboxed Development (Docker)

Section titled “5. Sandboxed Development (Docker)”

For enhanced security, use Docker isolation:

  • Controlled environment with persistent development state
  • Selective folder access without full filesystem exposure
  • Installed tools and configurations persist across sessions

Daily Workflow Examples

Section titled “Daily Workflow Examples”

Morning Code Review

Section titled “Morning Code Review”

Documentation Update

Section titled “Documentation Update”

Database Management

Section titled “Database Management”

File Organization

Section titled “File Organization”

Precautions & Security

Section titled “Precautions & Security”

Best Practices

Section titled “Best Practices”
  1. Use Docker for Isolation - Provides controlled environment
  2. Configure Command Blacklists - Restrict dangerous commands
  3. Set Directory Permissions - Limit access to specific folders
  4. Review Audit Logs - Monitor all executed commands
  5. Backup Before Operations - Especially for destructive tasks

Security Controls

Section titled “Security Controls”

Desktop Commander provides:

  • Command blacklist configuration
  • Directory access permissions
  • Audit logging for all operations
  • Telemetry opt-out options

Comparison with Similar Tools

Section titled “Comparison with Similar Tools”
FeatureDesktop CommanderMCP Filesystem ServerIDE Extensions
Terminal Control✅ Full❌ NoneLimited
File Search✅ RipgrepBasicVaries
Excel Support✅ Native❌ NonePlugin needed
PDF Support✅ Native❌ NonePlugin needed
Diff Editing✅ SurgicalBasic✅ Yes
Code Execution✅ In-memory❌ NoneVaries
Process Management✅ Full❌ NoneLimited
Docker Support✅ Yes❌ NoN/A
Auto-Updates✅ Options 1-4, 6ManualVia store

Recent Updates (2025)

Section titled “Recent Updates (2025)”
  • v0.1.40 (May 20) - Audit logging, improved line-based operations, enhanced edit_block
  • Fuzzy Search Logging (May 5) - Comprehensive logging for search operations
  • Telemetry Opt-out (April 29) - Configuration-based telemetry control
  • Better Code Search (March 25) - Enhanced context-aware results
  • WSL Support - Windows Subsystem for Linux exploration

Troubleshooting & FAQ

Section titled “Troubleshooting & FAQ”

Q: Commands are timing out

Section titled “Q: Commands are timing out”

A: Increase timeout in configuration or use background execution for long-running tasks.

Q: Permission denied errors

Section titled “Q: Permission denied errors”

A: Check directory access permissions in Desktop Commander configuration.

Q: Docker container not persisting data

Section titled “Q: Docker container not persisting data”

A: Use volume mounts (-v flag) for data persistence.

Q: Can’t find files with search

Section titled “Q: Can’t find files with search”

A: Ensure the search path is within allowed directories and try adjusting the search pattern.

Q: Updates not applying

Section titled “Q: Updates not applying”

A: For Options 1-4 and 6, restart Claude. For Option 5, run git pull && npm run setup.

Uninstallation

Section titled “Uninstallation”

Automatic (Recommended)

Section titled “Automatic (Recommended)”

Manual

Section titled “Manual”

Edit claude_desktop_config.json and remove the “desktop-commander” entry from mcpServers.

Community & Support

Section titled “Community & Support”
  • Website: desktopcommander.app
  • GitHub: wonderwhy-er/DesktopCommanderMCP
  • Discord: Join the Community
  • License: MIT (Free and Open Source)

Conclusion

Section titled “Conclusion”

Desktop Commander MCP represents a paradigm shift in how we interact with AI assistants for desktop automation. By providing Claude with comprehensive terminal control, file system access, and native data file support, it transforms Claude from a conversational AI into a powerful automation partner.

Whether you’re a developer looking to streamline your workflow, a data analyst needing quick insights, or a system administrator automating routine tasks, Desktop Commander MCP offers the tools you need. Its active development, growing community, and commitment to open-source principles make it an essential addition to any Claude Desktop setup.

Reference Links

Section titled “Reference Links”
  • Desktop Commander Official Website
  • GitHub Repository
  • Smithery Server Page
  • Discord Community
  • MCP Protocol Documentation
  • Claude Desktop Setup Guide

  • AI
  • MCP
  • Claude
  • Terminal
  • Automation
  • Desktop Commander
  • File System
  • Developer Tools

Desktop Commander MCP: Transform Claude into Your Ultimate AI Development Partner

Dec 22, 2025
Vibe Sparking

Desktop Commander MCP: Transform Claude into Your Ultimate AI Development Partner

Section titled “Desktop Commander MCP: Transform Claude into Your Ultimate AI Development Partner”

Introduction

Section titled “Introduction”

What if your AI assistant could not only understand your code but also execute commands, manage files, and work across your entire operating system? Desktop Commander MCP makes this possible, transforming Claude Desktop into a powerful, autonomous development partner that goes far beyond traditional IDE-focused AI tools.

Created by Eduard Ruzga (wonderwhy-er), Desktop Commander MCP is a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that bridges Claude Desktop with your local system. With over thousands of weekly npm downloads and a growing community of enthusiastic developers, it’s rapidly becoming the go-to solution for developers who want AI-assisted development without the fragmentation of multiple tools.

Key Features and Highlights

Section titled “Key Features and Highlights”

🚀 Enhanced Terminal Control

Section titled “🚀 Enhanced Terminal Control”

Desktop Commander provides intelligent terminal integration with:

  • Smart process detection - Automatically knows when programs are ready for input
  • Interactive session management - Maintain long-running sessions (SSH, databases, dev servers)
  • Background execution - Run commands with timeout support
  • Process control - List, monitor, and terminate processes

📁 Comprehensive File Operations

Section titled “📁 Comprehensive File Operations”
  • Full filesystem access with intelligent permissions
  • Native Excel support (.xlsx, .xls, .xlsm) - read, write, edit, search
  • PDF capabilities - Extract text, create from markdown, modify existing PDFs
  • Surgical code editing - Search/replace blocks for precise changes
  • Pattern-based replacements with vscode-ripgrep integration
  • URL support - Fetch content from web resources directly

💻 In-Memory Code Execution

Section titled “💻 In-Memory Code Execution”

Execute code without saving files:

🔧 Configuration Management

Section titled “🔧 Configuration Management”

Dynamic runtime configuration:

  • Blocked commands for security
  • Shell preferences (bash, zsh, PowerShell)
  • Directory access controls
  • File read/write limits

Prerequisites and Installation

Section titled “Prerequisites and Installation”

System Requirements

Section titled “System Requirements”
  • Node.js (v16 or higher recommended)
  • Claude Desktop application
  • Operating System: macOS, Windows, or Linux

Installation Options

Section titled “Installation Options”

Option 1: NPX (Recommended - Auto-Updates)

Section titled “Option 1: NPX (Recommended - Auto-Updates)”

Option 2: macOS Bash Script

Section titled “Option 2: macOS Bash Script”

Option 3: Docker (Complete Isolation)

Section titled “Option 3: Docker (Complete Isolation)”

Option 4: Manual Configuration

Section titled “Option 4: Manual Configuration”

Add to your claude_desktop_config.json:

Config locations:

  • macOS: ~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json
  • Windows: %APPDATA%\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json
  • Linux: ~/.config/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json

Recommended Scenarios and Daily Workflow

Section titled “Recommended Scenarios and Daily Workflow”

1. Codebase Exploration and Documentation

Section titled “1. Codebase Exploration and Documentation”

2. Multi-Project Development

Section titled “2. Multi-Project Development”

Unlike IDE tools limited to one workspace, Claude with Desktop Commander can:

  • Work across multiple repositories simultaneously
  • Compare implementations between projects
  • Copy patterns from one project to another

3. Automated DevOps Tasks

Section titled “3. Automated DevOps Tasks”

4. Data Analysis

Section titled “4. Data Analysis”

5. Interactive Development Environments

Section titled “5. Interactive Development Environments”

Best Practices

Section titled “Best Practices”

Security-First Configuration

Section titled “Security-First Configuration”
  1. Use dedicated configuration chats - Make config changes in separate conversations
  2. Set specific allowedDirectories - Limit filesystem access to needed paths
  3. Review blockedCommands - Prevent dangerous command execution
  4. Consider Docker for sensitive work - Complete isolation from host system

Efficient File Editing

Section titled “Efficient File Editing”

The edit_block tool uses search/replace blocks for surgical edits:

Pro Tips:

  • Make multiple small edits instead of large rewrites
  • Keep fileWriteLineLimit small to encourage efficient behavior
  • Use fuzzy search logs for debugging match failures

Session Management

Section titled “Session Management”

For long-running operations:

  1. Use start_process with smart ready detection
  2. Interact via interact_with_process
  3. Monitor with read_process_output
  4. Clean up with force_terminate

Comparison with Similar Tools

Section titled “Comparison with Similar Tools”
FeatureDesktop CommanderCursorWindsurf
ScopeEntire OSSingle projectSingle project
File ReadingFull filesChunkedChunked
Multi-Project✅ Yes❌ No❌ No
Terminal Control✅ Interactive⚠️ Limited⚠️ Limited
Cost ModelPro subscriptionAPI tokensAPI tokens
Token EconomyEfficientCan cascadeCan cascade

Why Developers Are Switching

Section titled “Why Developers Are Switching”

From the community testimonials:

“It’s a life saver! I paid for Claude + Cursor which always felt duplicated. This solves the problem ultimately.”

“Claude first to keep my sanity in check, then if necessary, engage with other IDEs.”

“I was able to compare old and new codebase fully, accounting for updates in the code, and fix issues in hours that I’d spent days on with other tools.”

Precautions and Troubleshooting

Section titled “Precautions and Troubleshooting”

Known Limitations

Section titled “Known Limitations”
  1. Directory restrictions can be bypassed - Security through allowedDirectories is not absolute
  2. Terminal commands unrestricted - allowedDirectories only affects file operations
  3. File write limits - Default 50 lines per write to encourage efficient AI behavior

Common Issues

Section titled “Common Issues”

Claude times out:

  • Ensure MCP server is running
  • Check config JSON syntax
  • Restart Claude Desktop

Commands fail:

  • Verify shell path in configuration
  • Check command isn’t in blockedCommands
  • Ensure proper permissions

File access denied:

  • Add path to allowedDirectories
  • Verify file permissions on disk

Debug Mode

Section titled “Debug Mode”

For troubleshooting:

Connect debugger to Node.js process on port 9229.

FAQ

Section titled “FAQ”

Q: Do I need API credits? A: No. Works with Claude Desktop Pro subscription ($20/month), not API calls.

Q: Does it auto-update? A: Yes, when installed via npx or Smithery, updates apply on Claude restart.

Q: Is my data collected? A: Limited pseudonymous telemetry only. No file contents or paths. Opt-out available.

Q: Can I use it with other Claude clients? A: Designed for Claude Desktop. Community adapters may exist for other clients.

Reference Links

Section titled “Reference Links”
  • GitHub Repository: wonderwhy-er/DesktopCommanderMCP
  • NPM Package: @wonderwhy-er/desktop-commander
  • Official Website: desktopcommander.app
  • Discord Community: Join Server
  • Video Tutorial: YouTube Setup Guide
  • Author’s Article: Claude with MCPs Replaced Cursor & Windsurf

Conclusion

Section titled “Conclusion”

Desktop Commander MCP represents a paradigm shift in AI-assisted development. By giving Claude direct access to your terminal and filesystem, it transforms conversational AI into an active development partner. Whether you’re exploring unfamiliar codebases, automating DevOps tasks, or building new features, Desktop Commander puts all your development tools in one intelligent chat interface.

The solution-centric approach—working with your entire OS rather than a single IDE workspace—combined with the cost efficiency of subscription-based pricing, makes it an compelling alternative to fragmented, API-token-hungry alternatives.

Ready to supercharge your development workflow? Install Desktop Commander and experience the future of AI-assisted development today.

Tags:
  • MCP
  • Claude
  • AI
  • Desktop Commander
  • Automation
  • Development
  • Terminal
  • File Management

Fabric: The Open-Source Framework for Augmenting Humans Using AI

Dec 22, 2025
Vibe Sparking

Fabric: The Open-Source Framework for Augmenting Humans Using AI

Section titled “Fabric: The Open-Source Framework for Augmenting Humans Using AI”

In the rapidly evolving landscape of AI tools, Fabric stands out as a revolutionary open-source framework designed to bridge the gap between AI capabilities and human integration. Created by security researcher and AI enthusiast Daniel Miessler, Fabric addresses a fundamental problem: AI doesn’t have a capabilities problem—it has an integration problem.

What is Fabric?

Section titled “What is Fabric?”

Fabric is an open-source framework that organizes and manages AI prompts—called Patterns—in a modular, reusable system. Unlike scattered prompts across various tools and platforms, Fabric provides a centralized, command-line-first approach to collecting, organizing, and executing AI solutions.

The Philosophy Behind Fabric

Section titled “The Philosophy Behind Fabric”

“AI isn’t a thing; it’s a magnifier of a thing. And that thing is human creativity.”

Fabric’s philosophy centers on breaking complex problems into manageable components and applying AI to each piece systematically. This approach transforms AI from a mysterious black box into a practical, everyday tool.

Key Features and Highlights

Section titled “Key Features and Highlights”

1. Pattern-Based Architecture

Section titled “1. Pattern-Based Architecture”

Patterns are the core building blocks of Fabric. Each Pattern is a carefully crafted prompt designed for a specific real-world task:

  • extract_wisdom: Extract key insights from YouTube videos and podcasts
  • summarize: Create concise summaries of any content
  • analyze_claims: Evaluate claims for accuracy and validity
  • write_essay: Generate essays in your own voice
  • create_coding_feature: Generate code features with context
  • improve_writing: Enhance writing quality and clarity

With over 150+ built-in Patterns, Fabric covers diverse use cases from content creation to code analysis.

2. Multi-Model Support

Section titled “2. Multi-Model Support”

Fabric supports an impressive array of AI providers:

  • OpenAI (GPT-4, GPT-4o, o1, o3)
  • Anthropic (Claude 4, Claude 4.5 Opus)
  • Google (Gemini)
  • Local models via Ollama
  • Azure OpenAI
  • Amazon Bedrock
  • Perplexity AI
  • Venice AI (privacy-focused)
  • Together AI
  • And many more…

3. Seamless CLI Integration

Section titled “3. Seamless CLI Integration”

Fabric is built for the command line, making it perfect for developers and power users:

4. Context and Session Management

Section titled “4. Context and Session Management”

Fabric supports persistent contexts and sessions:

  • Contexts: Pre-created background information added to all queries
  • Sessions: Maintain conversation history across multiple interactions
  • Variables: Dynamic pattern customization with {{variable}} syntax

5. REST API Server

Section titled “5. REST API Server”

Fabric includes a built-in REST API server for integration with other applications:

Features include:

  • Chat completions with streaming
  • Pattern management (CRUD operations)
  • YouTube transcript extraction
  • Swagger/OpenAPI documentation

6. Web Interface

Section titled “6. Web Interface”

A GUI alternative to the CLI is available for those who prefer visual interfaces, providing access to all Fabric functionality through a web browser.

Prerequisites

Section titled “Prerequisites”

Before installing Fabric, ensure you have:

  • Go 1.22+ (for source installation)
  • API keys for your preferred AI providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.)
  • yt-dlp (optional, for YouTube transcript extraction)
  • LaTeX distribution (optional, for PDF generation with to_pdf)

Installation

Section titled “Installation”

One-Line Install (Recommended)

Section titled “One-Line Install (Recommended)”

Unix/Linux/macOS:

Windows PowerShell:

Package Managers

Section titled “Package Managers”

macOS (Homebrew):

Arch Linux (AUR):

Windows (Winget):

From Source

Section titled “From Source”

Docker

Section titled “Docker”

Initial Setup

Section titled “Initial Setup”

After installation, run setup to configure your API keys:

Configuration

Section titled “Configuration”

Environment Variables

Section titled “Environment Variables”

Add to your shell configuration (~/.bashrc, ~/.zshrc):

Per-Pattern Model Mapping

Section titled “Per-Pattern Model Mapping”

Configure specific models for individual patterns:

Pattern Aliases

Section titled “Pattern Aliases”

Add convenient aliases for all patterns:

Best Practices and Daily Workflow Examples

Section titled “Best Practices and Daily Workflow Examples”

Content Creation Workflow

Section titled “Content Creation Workflow”

Video Learning Workflow

Section titled “Video Learning Workflow”

Code Review Workflow

Section titled “Code Review Workflow”

Security Analysis Workflow

Section titled “Security Analysis Workflow”

Daily Productivity

Section titled “Daily Productivity”

Custom Patterns

Section titled “Custom Patterns”

Create your own private patterns that won’t be overwritten during updates:

Comparison with Similar Tools

Section titled “Comparison with Similar Tools”
FeatureFabricLangChainOpenAI APIChatGPT
CLI-First✅❌❌❌
Pattern Library150+FrameworkNoneNone
Multi-Model✅✅OpenAI OnlyOpenAI Only
Local Models✅ (Ollama)✅❌❌
REST API✅❌✅❌
YouTube Integration✅❌❌❌
Custom Prompts✅✅✅Limited
Session Management✅✅❌✅

Troubleshooting

Section titled “Troubleshooting”

Common Issues

Section titled “Common Issues”

1. Command not found after installation:

2. API key errors:

3. YouTube transcripts not working:

4. Pattern not found:

5. Model not available:

FAQ

Section titled “FAQ”

Q: Is Fabric free to use? A: Yes, Fabric is MIT licensed. However, you need API keys for AI providers, which may have costs.

Q: Can I use Fabric without internet? A: Yes, with local models through Ollama.

Q: How do I contribute patterns? A: Submit a pull request to the Fabric repository with your pattern in the data/patterns directory.

Q: Does Fabric store my data? A: Fabric processes data locally. Data is sent to AI providers based on your configuration.

Q: Can I use multiple models in one workflow? A: Yes, you can chain patterns with different models using shell pipes.

Recent Updates (December 2025)

Section titled “Recent Updates (December 2025)”
  • v1.4.350: Interactive API Documentation with Swagger/OpenAPI UI
  • v1.4.338: Abacus vendor support for Chat-LLM models
  • v1.4.334: Claude Opus 4.5 model support
  • v1.4.322: Interactive HTML Concept Maps with Vis.js
  • v1.4.309: Comprehensive internationalization (9 languages)

Reference Links

Section titled “Reference Links”
  • GitHub Repository: github.com/danielmiessler/fabric
  • Pattern Library: Fabric Patterns
  • REST API Documentation: REST API Docs
  • Intro Videos:
    • Network Chuck Introduction
    • David Bombal Tutorial
    • Daniel Miessler’s Overview
  • Daniel Miessler’s Website: danielmiessler.com

Conclusion

Section titled “Conclusion”

Fabric represents a paradigm shift in how we interact with AI. By providing a structured, modular, and command-line-friendly framework, it transforms AI from an occasional tool into an integrated part of our daily workflows. Whether you’re a developer, content creator, security researcher, or knowledge worker, Fabric’s pattern-based approach offers a powerful way to augment your capabilities with AI.

The project’s active development, growing community, and extensive pattern library make it an essential tool for anyone serious about leveraging AI effectively. Start with the basic patterns, explore the community contributions, and eventually create your own—that’s the path to truly augmented human productivity.

This article was researched and written with assistance from AI tools, demonstrating the very integration that Fabric enables.

Tags:
  • AI
  • Fabric
  • Prompts
  • CLI
  • Automation
  • Productivity
  • Open Source
  • Daniel Miessler

Claude Code + MCP: Auto-Publish Articles to WeChat Official Account in Minutes

Dec 20, 2025
Vibe Sparking

Introduction

Section titled “Introduction”

What if you could go from a simple idea to a fully researched, professionally written article published to your WeChat Official Account - all in minutes, without leaving your terminal?

This isn’t science fiction. With Claude Code and the wenyan-mcp (Model Context Protocol) server, you can create an end-to-end content automation pipeline that:

  • Researches topics using web search
  • Generates high-quality bilingual articles
  • Automatically publishes to your WeChat draft box
  • Applies beautiful formatting themes

Let me show you exactly how to set this up.

What You’ll Need

Section titled “What You’ll Need”

Before we begin, make sure you have:

  1. Claude Code installed (v2.0+ recommended)
  2. Docker running on your machine
  3. WeChat Official Account with API credentials (AppID and AppSecret)

Step 1: Install Claude Code

Section titled “Step 1: Install Claude Code”

If you haven’t installed Claude Code yet:

Verify installation:

Step 2: Configure wenyan-mcp for WeChat

Section titled “Step 2: Configure wenyan-mcp for WeChat”

The magic happens through MCP (Model Context Protocol) - a standard that allows Claude to interact with external tools and services.

Add this configuration to your Claude Code MCP settings (~/.claude/settings.json or project-level .claude/settings.local.json):

Configuration Notes:

ParameterDescription
WECHAT_APP_IDYour WeChat Official Account AppID
WECHAT_APP_SECRETYour WeChat Official Account AppSecret
HOST_IMAGE_PATHLocal path for image storage (used for cover images)
-v mountMaps your local image directory to the container

Tip: Get your AppID and AppSecret from the WeChat Official Account Admin Platform.

Step 3: Create an Issue File

Section titled “Step 3: Create an Issue File”

The key to this workflow is defining your task in an issue file. This tells Claude exactly what you want to accomplish.

Create a file like engineering/00-issues/my-article-task.md:

Step 4: Execute the Workflow

Section titled “Step 4: Execute the Workflow”

Now comes the exciting part. Start Claude Code and reference your issue:

Then in the Claude Code prompt:

What Happens Next (Automatically)

Section titled “What Happens Next (Automatically)”

1. Research Phase

Section titled “1. Research Phase”

Claude searches the web for relevant, up-to-date information:

2. Content Synthesis

Section titled “2. Content Synthesis”

Claude fetches and analyzes multiple sources to create comprehensive content:

3. Article Generation & Publishing

Section titled “3. Article Generation & Publishing”

Claude generates the article and publishes directly to your WeChat draft box:

4. Review in WeChat

Section titled “4. Review in WeChat”

Open your WeChat Official Account admin panel - your article is waiting in the draft box:

Available Themes

Section titled “Available Themes”

The wenyan-mcp server supports multiple beautiful themes for your articles:

ThemeStyle
defaultClean, minimal
orangeheartWarm orange accents
rainbowColorful headings
lapisProfessional blue
piePlayful design
maizeGolden highlights
purpleElegant purple
phycatTech-focused

Specify your preferred theme when publishing:

Complete Workflow Summary

Section titled “Complete Workflow Summary”

Pro Tips

Section titled “Pro Tips”

1. Multi-Language Output

Section titled “1. Multi-Language Output”

Specify languages in your issue file:

Claude will generate both versions automatically.

2. Include Screenshots

Section titled “2. Include Screenshots”

Reference local images in your issue:

3. Customize Output Location

Section titled “3. Customize Output Location”

Specify where you want your blog files saved:

4. Review Before Final Publish

Section titled “4. Review Before Final Publish”

Articles go to your draft box first - you always have a chance to review and edit before final publication to subscribers.

Troubleshooting

Section titled “Troubleshooting”
IssueSolution
Docker connection failedEnsure Docker Desktop is running
WeChat API errorVerify AppID/AppSecret are correct
Images not uploadingCheck HOST_IMAGE_PATH is correctly mapped
MCP server not foundRun claude mcp list to verify configuration

Conclusion

Section titled “Conclusion”

This workflow transforms content creation from a tedious multi-step process into a streamlined, automated pipeline. With Claude Code + wenyan-mcp, you can:

  • Save hours on research and writing
  • Maintain consistency across bilingual content
  • Publish directly without copy-paste gymnastics
  • Focus on ideas rather than execution

The future of content creation is here - and it lives in your terminal.

References

Section titled “References”
  • Claude Code Documentation
  • wenyan-mcp GitHub Repository
  • MCP Protocol Specification
  • WeChat Official Account API

  • AI Agent
